items.prompt: Selection bias in this chapter means:
choices: Training data are not representative of the scored population
All groups have equal error by definition
Consent forms use the wrong font
items.explain: Example 3.18 shows error concentrating on groups scarce in training data.

items.prompt: Algorithmic bias can persist even when protected attributes are omitted because:
choices: Proxy features can carry demographic patterns
items.explain: Example 3.19 notes proxies in career history can reproduce hiring patterns.

items.prompt: Overall accuracy alone certifies fairness for high-stakes systems.
items.explain: Example 3.20 (COMPAS) stresses disparate error rates can persist under common thresholds.

items.prompt: Name two items from the chapter-level mitigation checklist.
items.rubric: Any two of: improve representation, audit subgroup error, document fairness criterion, apply matched mitigation, diverse review.
items.sample_answer: Audit subgroup error before release and choose a documented fairness criterion.
